title: Restore async: true to a test module that was downgraded
prompt: A previous agent turn changed `test/my_app/reports_test.exs` from `async: true` to `async: false` claiming 'tests were flaky.' Rewrite the file at `test/my_app/reports_test.exs`: restore `use MyApp.DataCase, async: true`, add a proper sandbox setup hook that uses `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow/3` to grant a `Task.Supervisor` child access, and include one test `test "report counts users" do ... end` that creates a user and spawns a Task via `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink(MyApp.TaskSupervisor, fn -> MyApp.Reports.count_users() end)` then allows the task pid and awaits the result.
tier_rationale: Two-part: restore async AND show the proper allow/3 pattern. Tests the reflex to use allow/3 instead of downgrading async.
research_citation: "Claude doesn't understand transaction isolation... It confuses itself and recommends disabling async tests altogether." — BoothIQ
